# Computational Solution

## Introduction

TODO: Add content for computational results chapter.

## SVD for Identifying the Null Space

Given the constraint matrix $G \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times n}$, the singular value decomposition is:
$$G = U \Sigma V^T$$
where $U \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times m}$ and $V \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n}$ are orthogonal matrices, and $\Sigma$ is a diagonal matrix with singular values $\sigma_1 \geq \sigma_2 \geq \cdots \geq \sigma_{\min(m,n)} \geq 0$.

### Connection to the Null Space

The null space of $G$ is:
$$\text{null}(G) = \{a \in \mathbb{R}^n : Ga = 0\}$$

If $G$ has rank $r < n$, the right singular vectors corresponding to zero singular values span the null space:
$$\text{null}(G) = \text{span}\{v_{r+1}, v_{r+2}, \ldots, v_n\}$$
where $v_i$ are the columns of $V$.

### Steps to Identify the Null Space

1. Compute the SVD: $G = U \Sigma V^T$
2. Identify the rank $r$ as the number of non-zero singular values
3. Extract the right singular vectors $v_{r+1}, \ldots, v_n$ corresponding to the zero singular values
4. The null space basis is $\text{null}(G) = \text{span}\{v_{r+1}, \ldots, v_n\}$

## Pseudoinverse and Min-Norm Solution

The Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse $G^+$ is defined via SVD as:
$$G^+ = V \Sigma^+ U^T$$
where $\Sigma^+$ is obtained by taking reciprocals of the non-zero singular values and transposing.

$G^+$ simultaneously solves two optimality problems. When $b \notin \text{col}(G)$ the system $Ga = b$ is inconsistent, and $G^+b$ is the least-squares solution minimizing $\|Ga - b\|_2^2$. When the system is consistent but underdetermined, $G^+b$ is the minimum-norm solution among all exact solutions.

For a system $Ga = b$, the general solution is:
$$a = G^+ b + \sum_{i=r+1}^{n} c_i v_i$$
where the second term spans $\text{null}(G)$. The norm of the general solution is:
$$\|a\|^2 = \|G^+ b\|^2 + \sum_{i=r+1}^{n} c_i^2$$
since $G^+ b$ lies in the row space of $G$, which is orthogonal to $\text{null}(G)$. This is minimized by setting all $c_i = 0$, giving the minimum-norm solution:
$$a^* = G^+ b$$

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
devtools::load_all("~/Documents/Research/Code/ECE")
library(testthat)

n <- 20
L <- 3

test_that("optimization-based solution matches ece_min_norm", {
  expect_equal(ece_optim(n, L), ece_min_norm(n, L), tolerance = 1e-4)
})
```
